chunk_summary: This section outlines the core competencies required for residents and team members delivering tele-nutrition services, focusing on digital tools, telemedicine guidelines, and training frameworks for effective implementation.
learning_objectives: Understand the core competencies required for tele-nutrition team members.
Familiarize with the Telemedicine Practice Guidelines (India) and WHO recommendations on digital health interventions.
Learn the role of the National Commission for Allied and Healthcare Professions Act, 2021 in tele-nutrition training.
Identify key digital tools and frameworks for tele-nutrition delivery.
key_facts: The Telemedicine Practice Guidelines (India) provide a framework for delivering tele-nutrition services effectively.
The WHO guidelines emphasize monitoring and evaluating digital health interventions for improved health outcomes.
The National Commission for Allied and Healthcare Professions Act, 2021, supports the regulation and training of healthcare professionals in tele-nutrition.
Digital tools like eSanjeevani are integral to tele-nutrition delivery in India.
Tele-nutrition training must include competencies in patient communication, digital literacy, and adherence to telemedicine regulations.
reference_quote: The Telemedicine Practice Guidelines (India) provide a structured framework for delivering telemedicine services, including tele-nutrition, ensuring compliance with legal and ethical standards.
document_key_topics: WHO recommendations on digital health interventions
